title: Timeout Layers, Deadlines, Cancellation, Cleanup, and Budget Allocation · Automation and Data Clie… · Language Clinic: Cancellation Cleanup
summary: A nightly reconciliation has a fixed run window and must divide deadlines across resolution, connect, headers, streamed body, parsing, and cleanup. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http py cancellation cleanup increment in a Python 3.14 urllib and HTTP client layers end-to-end request budget and cancellation model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Stop body processing, producers, timers, and queued work after cancellation and release every owned resource. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Allocate an end-to-end latency budget across attempts and phases while preserving time for cleanup and fallback. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""http-clients-python cumulative safe data-client portfolio.

Browser labs use deterministic request and response models only. Live urllib,
DNS, TLS, sockets, concurrency, and load require local Python 3.14 transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RequestEvidence:
    method: str
    target: str
    attempt: int

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ResponseEvidence:
    status: int
    body_bytes: int
    valid: bool

print("Python HTTP client portfolio ready")
